chunks.text: EECS Cal Day - EECS at Berkeley (Photo: Adam Lau) EECS Cal Day EECS students, faculty, and staff host a variety of events on Cal Day to introduce newly admitted students and their families to our community. Cal Day 2026 will be dedicated to registered, newly admitted students and their families only. Saturday, April 18, 2026 Cal Day In-Person Events EECS Advisor Tabling Engineering Information Zone Meet your Electrical Engineering & Computer Science Advisors! They’re here to help you as you navigate your journey through this amazing degree program. Ask questions about requirements and how to get involved as a student. We look forward to seeing you there! Grimes Center, Raw Thrills Lounge 11:30am – 2:30pm CS Advisor Tabling Northwest Information Zone Meet your Computer Science Advisors! They’re here to help you as you navigate your journey through this amazing degree program. Ask questions about requirements and how to get involved as a student. We look forward to seeing you there! Location TBD 10am – 1pm Student Sessions Kickstarting Your Journey as a Woman in CS! Find your community of women interested in computer science, learn about a free EECS-sponsored residential CS bootcamp with an Amazon Field Trip, and make bracelets while chatting with alumni about what Berkeley CS is like!! We welcome female-identifying admits of all majors interested in learning CS skills, no prior experience required! Women in Tech Panel and Lunch with AWE Congratulations on your admission to Cal! To celebrate, the Association of Women in Electrical Engineering

bracelets while chatting with alumni about what Berkeley CS is like!! We welcome female-identifying admits of all majors interested in learning CS skills, no prior experience required! Women in Tech Panel and Lunch with AWE Congratulations on your admission to Cal! To celebrate, the Association of Women in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science (AWE) invites you to a panel on Cal Day! Here you can learn about what it’s like to be a Berkeley CS/EECS student, learn about our organization, find out about opportunities, and get all your questions answered with lunch provided. RSVP ! Computer Science Mentors: Intro to the Berkeley EE/CS Community Computer Science Mentors (CSM) is a student-run organization that provides small group tutoring sessions to help students gain a deeper understanding of concepts and develop a feeling of community. Join us as we introduce the EE/CS program at Berkeley – how to navigate lower-division courses, how to find a welcoming community, etc – and how CSM fits into it! Welcome to Machine Learning at Berkeley! Join us for an intro to Machine Learning at Berkeley, some advice on being a Berkeley engineer, and a panel with current students pursuing machine learning and AI! The Art of Creating an Animated Film In this event we will be showing you how we create an animated short film in an hour going through the whole pipeline using Autodesk Maya! How Berkeley Designs & Builds Chips: Courses, Research, and Student Projects Wozniak Lounge 10am-12pm Join us for an overview of

Creating an Animated Film In this event we will be showing you how we create an animated short film in an hour going through the whole pipeline using Autodesk Maya! How Berkeley Designs & Builds Chips: Courses, Research, and Student Projects Wozniak Lounge 10am-12pm Join us for an overview of hardware and chip design at Berkeley! We’ll go over core chip design coursework, our capstone tapeout class where students build real silicon, and opportunities to get involved in silicon research. You’ll see past student-designed chips and learn about additional opportunities such as national conferences, industry partnerships, and hands-on opportunities beyond the classroom. Student Group Tabling Meet some of the amazing student organizations from Electrical Engineering and Computer Science! Participating student organizations: CS Mentors ** (CSM)** CS Kickstart (CSK) Computer Science Undergraduate Association (CSUA) Association of Women in Engineering (AWE) Eta Kappa Nu, EECS Honors Society (HKN) Machine Learning at Berkeley (ML@B) Hispanic Engineers and Scientists (HES) Black Engineering and Science Student Association (BESSA) Institute of Electronics and Electronics Engineers (IEEE) Society of Women Engineers (SWE) *Please note that EECS student groups will be tabling in two separate locations: the Soda Hall Breezeway and the Grimes Center/Mclaughlin Breezeway. Faculty Presentations CS Major Information Session Join us for an engaging presentation with the Computer Science Division in the EECS Department, where you will learn about the department, academics, and exciting enrichment opportunities! EECS Major Information Session Join us for an engaging presentation with the EECS Department, where you will learn about the

Major Information Session Join us for an engaging presentation with the Computer Science Division in the EECS Department, where you will learn about the department, academics, and exciting enrichment opportunities! EECS Major Information Session Join us for an engaging presentation with the EECS Department, where you will learn about the department, academics, and exciting enrichment opportunities! Student Demonstrations GamesCrafters GamesCrafters is a computational game theory research and development group led by Teaching Professor Dan Garcia. Our GAMESMAN system strongly solves two-person, abstract strategy games (e.g., Tic-Tac-Toe and Connect Four) and one-person puzzles (e.g., Lights Out and Peg Solitaire) for play and analysis. Come on by to play with it and meet our GamesCrafters! EECS Student Panel EE&CS Student Life Panel Join our student panelists to learn more about campus life as an Electrical Engineering or Computer Science student! From academics and clubs, to research and professional experiences, get a glimpse into what your future could be here at Cal. Accessibility If you require an accommodation for effective communication or information about campus mobility access features in order to fully participate in our virtual events, please contact Grayson Johnston (gjohnston@berkeley.edu) with as much advance notice as possible and at least 7 days in advance of the event.
